---
title: "steam missing libGL.so.1"
date: 2013-05-07
forum: New to Ubuntu
---

### Post by My Will My Truth on 2013-05-07
I just installed steam on ubuntu 13.04 64bit in a laptop with Optimus Nvidia technology, and got a "You are missing the following 32-bit libraries, and Steam may not run: libGL.so.1" and "Fatal error: unable to load steamui.so"
I have bumblebee installed. What can I do?

---

### Post by Kopkins on 2013-05-07
Try:
```
sudo apt-get install libgl1-mesa-glx libgl1-mesa-dev
```

Kopkins

---

### Post by My Will My Truth on 2013-05-15
Thanks.
I got this:

Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree       
Reading state information... Done
libgl1-mesa-glx is already the newest version.
You might want to run 'apt-get -f install' to correct these:
The following packages have unmet dependencies:
 libgl1-mesa-dev : Depends: mesa-common-dev (= 9.1.1-0ubuntu3) but it is not going to be installed
                   Depends: libdrm-dev (>= 2.4.24) but it is not going to be installed
                   Depends: libx11-dev but it is not going to be installed
                   Depends: libx11-xcb-dev but it is not going to be installed
                   Depends: libxcb-dri2-0-dev but it is not going to be installed
                   Depends: libxcb-glx0-dev but it is not going to be installed
                   Depends: libxdamage-dev but it is not going to be installed
                   Depends: libxext-dev but it is not going to be installed
                   Depends: libxfixes-dev but it is not going to be installed
                   Depends: libxxf86vm-dev but it is not going to be installed
                   Depends: x11proto-dri2-dev (>= 2.6) but it is not going to be installed
                   Depends: x11proto-gl-dev (>= 1.4.14) but it is not going to be installed
 primus-libs-ia32:i386 : Depends: primus-libs:i386 but it is not going to be installed
E: Unmet dependencies. Try 'apt-get -f install' with no packages (or specify a solution).

### Post by My Will My Truth on 2013-05-15
"Check if your using third party repositories. If so disable them, since they are a common source of problems. Furthermore run the following command in a terminal: apt-get install -f"

### Post by My Will My Truth on 2013-05-15

Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree       
Reading state information... Done
You might want to run 'apt-get -f install' to correct these.

anonymous@anon:~$ sudo apt-get install -f
Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree

Errors were encountered while processing:
 /var/cache/apt/archives/primus-libs_20130511-1~raringppa1_i386.deb

### Post by pqwoerituytrueiwoq on 2013-05-15
Try doing this and see if it works, it said it needs the **32bit** library
```
sudo apt-get install libgl1-mesa-glx:i386
```

---

### Post by My Will My Truth on 2013-05-15
anonymous@anon:~$ sudo apt-get install libgl1-mesa-glx:i386
Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree       
Reading state information... Done
libgl1-mesa-glx:i386 is already the newest version.
libgl1-mesa-glx:i386 set to manually installed.
You might want to run 'apt-get -f install' to correct these:
The following packages have unmet dependencies:

### Post by My Will My Truth on 2013-05-15
Before breaking the Packages Manager I followed steps from [www.webupd8.org/2012/11/primus-better-performance-and-less.htmlCCs](www.webupd8.org/2012/11/primus-better-performance-and-less.htmlCCs) and this:
[h=3]"Advanced Setups[/h] For advanced users, if you do not want to use the proprietary nvidia driver or 32-bit libraries (for example, if you are only interested in power savings), you can do your custom installation. 

Minimal setup : sudo apt-get install --no-install-recommends bumblebee 

Depending on your needs, add to this line: 


[LIST]
[*]bumblebee-nvidia: proprietary nvidia driver support (if installed, become default over nouveau) 


[*]virtualgl: VirtualGL as backend 


[*]virtualgl-libs-ia32: 32bit support for VirtualGL on 64bit system, necessary to run 32bit app through optirun 


[*]primus: primus/primusrun as backend (virtualgl Stays default, you need to run optirun -b primus <app>) 


[*]primus-libs-ia32: 32bit support for primus/primurun on 64bit system, necessary to run 32bit app through optirun"


^From [https://wiki.ubuntu.com/Bumblebee](https://wiki.ubuntu.com/Bumblebee)

[/LIST]

---

libgl1-mesa-glx:i386 set to manually installed.
You might want to run '**apt-get -f install**' to correct these:
The following packages have unmet dependencies:
 primus-libs-ia32:i386 : Depends: primus-libs:i386 but it is not going to be installed
E: Unmet dependencies. Try 'apt-get -f install' with no packages (or specify a solution).
so try running 
```
sudo apt-get -f install
```

### Post by ptn107 on 2013-05-16
Did you get steam from here?:
[_[http://media.steampowered.com/client/installer/steam.deb](http://media.steampowered.com/client/installer/steam.deb)_]("http://media.steampowered.com/client/installer/steam.deb")

This is all I needed to install on 13.04 x64.

and
```
sudo dpkg -i steam_latest.deb; sudo apt-get -f install
```

There is no need to install steam from a third party location.

---

### Post by Xynche on 2013-05-17
I had primus running fine a few days back but desired to do a clean install of Ubuntu after experimenting with a lot that I was uncomfortable with. I'm having the same problem, it's almost as if the ppa:bumblebee/stable is broken
```
Unpacking primus-lib (from .../primus-lib_201303160901git~precise1_amd64.deb) ...
dpkg: error processing /var/cache/apt/archives/primus-lib_201303160901git~precise1_amd64.deb (--unpack):
 trying to overwrite '/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/primus/libGL.so.1', which is also in package primus-libs 20130511-1~preciseppa1
Selecting previously unselected package primus.
Unpacking primus (from .../primus_201303160901git~precise1_amd64.deb) ...
Errors were encountered while processing:
 /var/cache/apt/archives/primus-lib_201303160901git~precise1_amd64.deb
E: Sub-process /usr/bin/dpkg returned an error code (1)

```
The problem looks like the primus-lib files I'm getting from the ppa. If I remove the repositories and start the update it runs a partial update which allows me to use primusrun on glxgears and what not. Unfortunatly, it breaks on any 'real' programs/games. Minecraft is the only thing I have at the moment to test and I can't run it with```
optirun java -jar minecraft.jar
```which is no suprise```
optirun bash
java -jar minecraft.jar
```has always been the way I got it to work, and it does work fine. However primusrun isn't as easy. Any fix would be great, I just want primus working again!

---

### Post by Xynche on 2013-05-17
I found a fix for primus.

[http://www.absolutelytech.com/2010/06/30/solved-error-dpkg-error-processing-filename-unpack-trying-to-overwrite/](http://www.absolutelytech.com/2010/06/30/solved-error-dpkg-error-processing-filename-unpack-trying-to-overwrite/)

Java probably is borken on my machine, but primus is fine now!

---

### Post by Utram on 2013-07-16
This seem to happen on every 64bits OS. Full bug report here:

[https://github.com/ValveSoftware/steam-for-linux/issues/321](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/steam-for-linux/issues/321)

Solution:

sudo gedit /etc/ld.so.conf.d/steam.conf

Add next two lines to file:

/usr/lib32
/usr/lib/i386-linux-gnu/mesa

Then execute:

sudo ldconfig

Now you should be able to run Steam

original post: [http://steamcommunity.com/app/221410/discussions/0/864959336441792994/#c864959336494129639](http://steamcommunity.com/app/221410/discussions/0/864959336441792994/#c864959336494129639)

---

### Post by Sn3ipen on 2013-12-04
> **Utram said:**
> This seem to happen on every 64bits OS. Full bug report here:

[https://github.com/ValveSoftware/steam-for-linux/issues/321](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/steam-for-linux/issues/321)

Solution:

sudo gedit /etc/ld.so.conf.d/steam.conf

Add next two lines to file:

/usr/lib32
/usr/lib/i386-linux-gnu/mesa

Then execute:

sudo ldconfig

Now you should be able to run Steam

original post: [http://steamcommunity.com/app/221410/discussions/0/864959336441792994/#c864959336494129639](http://steamcommunity.com/app/221410/discussions/0/864959336441792994/#c864959336494129639)

&#8599; This worked. &#8599;

Thanks!

---

### Post by efflandt on 2013-12-05
In 64-bit 13.10 I had to add a file /etc/ld.so.conf.d/steam.conf with paths for steam to find that 32-bit file. For more info see #3 in [http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=2188201](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=2188201)

I heard that 13.10 had better support for bumblebee already included, but I did have to do some things and install some other 32-bit libs to get steam (using Intel) and a game (TF2 using nvidia) working (Intel HD 4600/Nvidia GTX 765M). I got graphics to work on nvidia using **optirun** (the game needed additional parameters), but nothing other than glxinfo works with **primusrun** (maybe due to nvidia-experimental-310 which is actually 319.60). I got no graphics (no X) after temporarily installing **nvidia-prime** (no keyboard/mouse response in dialog asking if I wanted to use low res graphics or reconfigure), so I purged nvidia-prime from text console.

---

### Post by mickmize on 2014-03-14
Post #21 worked for me, too! Thanks, Ultram!

I'm on  a brand-new install of 12.04 with Bumblebee.

---
